name: blacksmith

metadata.name: Blacksmith
metadata.summary: REST API Client designed for microservices

documentation:
# Blacksmith

```{image} https://github.com/mardiros/blacksmith/actions/workflows/publish-doc.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Documentation
:target: https://mardiros.github.io/blacksmith/user/introduction.html
```

```{image} https://github.com/mardiros/blacksmith/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Continuous Integration
:target: https://github.com/mardiros/blacksmith/actions/workflows/main.yml
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/mardiros/blacksmith/branch/master/graph/badge.svg?token=17KAC0LW9H
:alt: Coverage Report
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/mardiros/blacksmith
```

```{image} https://api.codeclimate.com/v1/badges/4d36d0011afda749039a/maintainability
:alt: Maintainability
:target: https://codeclimate.com/github/mardiros/blacksmith/maintainability
```

Blacksmith is a library to build a solid microservices architecture based on REST API.

Todays, developers have lots of choices to create microservices,
plenty of framework are available, but when it comes to consume them,
there is a lack of clients.

Consuming an API is not just about doing HTTP requests, it has to be designed
for failure, monitoring, and service discovery with an elegant abstraction.
blacksmith aims to provide a solution for developers to write clean client code
and for ops to monitor api calls also on the client side.

## What is Blacksmith

Blacksmith is a declarative tool for consuming REST Api.

In a REST API, resources are declared under HTTP routes, and every http verb
has its own definition.

In Blacksmith, every resources are bound to schemas that define request and response,
in order to abstract HTTP.

This is a common concept for SQL table with ORM, where tables are bound to models,
and then, operations are available on models. This is a usefull abstraction to
write maintainable code and to dive into a project easilly.

Handling API resources using an http client, such as [requests] does not handle
that abstraction, and does not handle bindings to objects, and can be compared to
a raw connection because it is just a transport.

This is the problem blacksmith is trying to solve, having a nice abstraction of
services that use REST in a microservices architecture.

:::{note}
Blacksmith is not an HTTP Client or a model validator.

Blacksmith use 

[httpx]

 to perform http query, and use 

[Pydantic]

 to validate models.
:::

## Why not using a SDK to consume APIs ?

SDK are about importing an external library in a service. And a service is
consumed by many services for different purpose. As a result, SDK create
coupling between service, and this is something that should be avoid.

An SDK for a service will declare all the resources, routes, and attribute
of resources when a service consumer may consume just a few.

SDK may hide what is really used by every service.

To avoid this, every consumers of API, should declare its own consumers
contracts to get a better view of which service use what.

:::{note}
TLDR; SDK are fine in public API, by the way, but not in a microservices
architecture.
:::

## Building SDK

By the way, blacksmith can be use to build a SDK for public API.

## Working with or without asyncio

Blacksmith is written for `asyncio`, but works with synchronous API without overhead.
The synchronous API is generated using [unasync] and tested.

:::{note}
All class started by `Async` are asynchronous, and all class started by `Sync` are
synchronous, for an explicit usage
:::

## Read More

You can read the [full documentation of this library here].

:::{important}
The documentation has been moved to github pages.

The documentation under readthedocs is obsolete.
:::

[full documentation of this library here]: https://mardiros.github.io/blacksmith/user/introduction.html
[httpx]: https://www.python-httpx.org/
[pydantic]: https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/
[requests]: https://docs.python-requests.org/
[unasync]: https://unasync.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

modules.name: blacksmith.middleware._sync.circuit_breaker
documentation: Cut the circuit in case a service is down.

classes.name: blacksmith.middleware._sync.circuit_breaker.SyncCircuitBreakerMiddleware
documentation:
Prevent cascading failure.

The circuit breaker is based on [purgatory], the middleware create
one circuit breaker per client_name. The parameters are forwarded
to all the clients. This middleware does not give the possibility to
adapt a threshold or the time the circuit is opened per clients.

[purgatory]: https://pypi.org/project/purgatory-circuitbreaker/

Zipkin Middleware based on an abstract context manager.

```
param trace

A deferred context manager that manage the trace span stack.
```

modules.name: blacksmith.middleware._sync.prometheus
documentation: Collect metrics based on prometheus.

classes.name: blacksmith.middleware._sync.prometheus.SyncPrometheusMiddleware
documentation:
Collect the api calls made in a prometheus registry.

It expose a `blacksmith_info` Gauge to get the blacksmith version, as a label,
and a `blacksmith_request_latency_seconds_count` Counter to get the number of
http requests made.
The counter `blacksmith_request_latency_seconds_count` as client_name, method,
path and status_code labels.

:::{note}
the service_name and service version is redundant with the client_name,
so they are not exposed as labels. By the way, you may have multiple
client_name for 1 service name/version.
:::

modules.name: blacksmith.domain.scanner
documentation:
Convenient method to fillout an blacksmith registry.

```
import blacksmith
blacksmith.scan('mypkg.resources', 'other.resources')
```

functions.name: blacksmith.domain.scanner.scan
documentation:
Collect all resources to fillout the registry.

Basically, it import modules registered using {func}`blacksmith.register`.

```
raises TypeError

malformed module name

raises ModuleNotFoundError

unknown package name

raises AttributeError

argument is a module, not a package.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.registry.Registry.register
documentation:
Register the resource in the registry.

```
param client_name

used to identify the client in your code.

param resource

name of the resource in your code.

param service

name of the service in the service discovery.

param version

version number of the service.

param path

endpoint of the resource in the given service.

param contract

contract for the resource, define request and response.

param collection_path

endpoint of the resource collection
in the given service.

param collection_contract

contract for the resource collection,
define request and response.
```

This method is used to find the endpoint of the service.
Store resources in a registry.

SyncClientFactory and AsyncClientFactory.

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.middleware.http_cache.CacheControlPolicy
documentation:
Initialize the caching using `Cache-Control` http headers.
Also consume the `Vary` response header to cache response per
Vary response headers per request.

```
param sep

Separator used in cache key MUST NOT BE USED in client name.
```

functions.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.parse_header_links
documentation:
Returns a list of parsed link headers, for more info see:
<https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Link>

The generic syntax of those is:

```
Link: < uri-reference >; param1=value1; param2="value2"
```

So for instance:

Link; '\<<http:/.../front.jpeg>>; type="image/jpeg",\<<http://.../back.jpeg>>;'
would return

```
[
    {"url": "http:/.../front.jpeg", "type": "image/jpeg"},
    {"url": "http://.../back.jpeg"},
]
```

:::{note}
Stolen code from httpx \_utils.py (private method)
:::

```
param value

HTTP Link entity-header field

return

list of parsed link headers
```

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPTimeout
documentation: Request timeout.

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPRequest
documentation:
Internal representation of an http request.

Note that the HTTP method is not present, because the method is
the funcion called.

The HTTP Request is filled out using the
{class}`blacksmith.domain.model.params.Request` schema.

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPRawResponse

class_variables.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPRawResponse.headers
documentation:
The headers response implmentation should be key insensitive, http standard.

Blacksmith rely on httpx as the default implementation, key are insensitive.

Internal representation of an http response.
This format is used to deserialize the response body to the HTTPResponse.

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPResponse

class_variables.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPResponse.status_code
documentation: HTTP Status code.

class_variables.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPResponse.headers
documentation: Header of the response.

class_variables.name: blacksmith.domain.model.http.HTTPResponse.json
documentation: Json Body of the response.

Intermediate representation of an http response.

In this representation, the response body has been parsed to the property `json`,
which is a python structure containing simple python types. This http response
representation will be used create pydantic response object.

classes.name: blacksmith.service._sync.client.SyncClient

methods.name: blacksmith.service._sync.client.SyncClient.__getattr__
documentation:
The client has attributes that are the registered resource.

The resource are registered using the {func}`blacksmith.register` function.

Client representation for the client name.

A client will have dymanic property, based on the registered resources.

classes.name: blacksmith.service._sync.client.SyncClientFactory

methods.name: blacksmith.service._sync.client.SyncClientFactory.add_middleware
documentation:
Add a middleware to the client factory and return the client for chaining.

..note:: Clients created before the call of this method will also be
    altered. The middleware stack is a reference for all clients.

Client creator, for the given configuration.

```
param sd

Service Discovery instance

param transport

HTTP Client that process the call,
default use 

param timeout

configure timeout,
this parameter is ignored if the transport has been passed

param proxies

configure proxies,
this parameter is ignored if the transport has been passed

param verify_certificate

Reject request if certificate are invalid for https

param collection_parser

use to customize the collection parser
default use 
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.AbstractCollectionParser.meta
documentation:
Return the metatadata from the response.

Usually, metadata are in a header, but if the API wrap the list,

```
{
    "total_items": 0,
    "items": []
}
```

Then, the `Metadata.total_count` can be extracted from the json,
instead of the header.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.AbstractCollectionParser.json

documentation: Signature of the collection parser.

classes.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.json
documentation:
Return the raw json response.

It return the raw response body without noticing if its a
normal or an error response.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.as_result
documentation:
Return the result as a `result.Result`.

The {class}`blacksmith.ResponseBox` mimic the `result.Result` of the
{term}`result library`, but, you may want to cast the response box as a result.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.as_optional
documentation:
Expose the instance as an optional result.

In case no response schema has been provided while registering the resource,
then a `Ok(None)` is return to not raise any
{class}`blacksmith.NoResponseSchemaException`

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.is_ok
documentation: Return True if the response was an http success.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.is_err
documentation: Return True if the response was an http error.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.unwrap
documentation:
Return the parsed response.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.unwrap_err
documentation: Return the response error.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.unwrap_or
documentation:
Return the response or the default value in case of error.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.unwrap_or_else
documentation:
Return the response or the callable return in case of error.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.unwrap_or_raise
documentation:
Return the response or raise the exception exc.

```
raises exc

it the response is an error.

raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.expect
documentation:
Return the response or raise an UnwrapError exception with the given message.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.expect_err
documentation: Return the error or raise an UnwrapError exception with the given message.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.map
documentation:
Apply op on response in case of success, and return the new result.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.map_or
documentation:
Apply and return op on response in case of success, default in case of error.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.map_or_else
documentation:
Return the result of default_op in case of error otherwise the result of op.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.map_err
documentation:
Apply op on error in case of error, and return the new result.

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.and_then
documentation:
Apply the op function on the response and return it if success

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.or_else
documentation:
Apply the op function on the error and return it if error

```
raises NoResponseSchemaException

if there are no response schema set.
```

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.inspect
documentation: Call op with the contained value if `Ok` and return the original result.

methods.name: blacksmith.domain.model.params.ResponseBox.inspect_err
documentation: Call op with the contained error if `Ok` and return the original result.

Get the response metadata such as counts in http header, links...

Those metadata are generated by the collection_parser.
Deserialize the models in a json response list, item by item.

functions.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.register_http_body_serializer
documentation: Register a serializer to serialize some kind of request.

functions.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.unregister_http_body_serializer
documentation:
Unregister a serializer previously added.

Usefull for testing purpose.

functions.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.serialize_request_body
documentation:
Serialize the body of the request.

Note that the content_type is optional, but if it is set,

the request will contains

functions.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.serialize_request
documentation:
Serialize {class}`blacksmith.Request` subclasses to {class}`blacksmith.HTTPRequest`.

While processing an http request, the request models are serialize to an
intermediate object {class}`blacksmith.HTTPRequest`, that will be passed over
middleware and finally to the transport in order to build the final http request.

Note that the body of the {class}`blacksmith.HTTPRequest` is a string, here,
serialized by a registered serializer.

functions.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.serialize_response
documentation:
Deserialize an http response to the http intermediate representation that will
become the pydantic based response.
Basically it parse json bytes a a python structure. But this function is here
to supports serializations format depending on the content-type.

classes.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.AbstractHttpBodySerializer

methods.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.AbstractHttpBodySerializer.accept
documentation: Return true in case it can handle the request.

methods.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.AbstractHttpBodySerializer.serialize
documentation:
Serialize a python simple types to a python request body.

The body received here is the extracted object from the request model.

methods.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.AbstractHttpBodySerializer.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize a raw http response body to a python simple types representation.
Request body serializer.

classes.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.JsonRequestSerializer
documentation: The default serializer that serialize to json

classes.name: blacksmith.service.http_body_serializer.UrlencodedRequestSerializer
documentation: A serializer for application/x-www-form-urlencoded request.

classes.name: blacksmith.service._async.client.AsyncClient

methods.name: blacksmith.service._async.client.AsyncClient.__getattr__
documentation:
The client has attributes that are the registered resource.

The resource are registered using the {func}`blacksmith.register` function.

Client representation for the client name.

A client will have dymanic property, based on the registered resources.

classes.name: blacksmith.service._async.client.AsyncClientFactory

methods.name: blacksmith.service._async.client.AsyncClientFactory.add_middleware
documentation:
Add a middleware to the client factory and return the client for chaining.

..note:: Clients created before the call of this method will also be
    altered. The middleware stack is a reference for all clients.

Client creator, for the given configuration.

```
param sd

Service Discovery instance

param transport

HTTP Client that process the call,
default use 

param timeout

configure timeout,
this parameter is ignored if the transport has been passed

param proxies

configure proxies,
this parameter is ignored if the transport has been passed

param verify_certificate

Reject request if certificate are invalid for https

param collection_parser

use to customize the collection parser
default use 
```

classes.name: blacksmith.service._sync.route_proxy.SyncRouteProxy

methods.name: blacksmith.service._sync.route_proxy.SyncRouteProxy.collection_head
documentation: Use to perform an http `HEAD` query on the collection_path.

methods.name: blacksmith.service._sync.route_proxy.SyncRouteProxy.collection_get
documentation:
Retrieve a collection of resources.

It perform an http `GET` query on the collection_path.

The collection is return in as an iterator, and models ares validated one
by one using the `TCollectionResponse` schema which descrine one item
of the collection.

:::{important}
This method is the only method that behave as an iterator.
You can update the way collection are deserialize for a whole client,
by passing a {class}`blacksmith.AbstractCollectionParser` on the
{class}`blacksmith.AsyncClientFactory` (
or {class}`blacksmith.SyncClientFactory` for the synchronous version).
:::

modules.name: blacksmith.sd._sync.adapters.router
documentation: The Server-Side Service discovery strategy.

classes.name: blacksmith.sd._sync.adapters.router.SyncRouterDiscovery

methods.name: blacksmith.sd._sync.adapters.router.SyncRouterDiscovery.get_endpoint
documentation:
Create and return the endpoint using the given parameters `service_url_fmt`
or `unversioned_service_url_fmt` if version is `None`.

Router that implement a {term}`Server-Side Service discovery`.

This implementation never raise
{class}`blacksmith.domain.exceptions.UnregisteredServiceException`
when service are unknown, because it only passe very request to the router
server that is connected to the {term}`service registry`.

:::{note}
Given pattern in parameter have to match the format of the router server.
:::

```
param service_url_fmt

A pattern used to create endpoint of versionned services.

param unversioned_service_url_fmt

A pattern used to create endpoint of
unversionned services.
```

Get the {class}`Service` from the consul registry.

If many instances host the service, the host is choosen randomly.

documentation:
Get the endpoint from the consul registry

If many instances host the service, the host is choosen randomly.

A discovery instance based on a {term}`Consul` server.

```
param addr

endpoint of the consul v1 http api.

param service_name_fmt

pattern for name of versionned service.

param service_url_fmt

pattern for url of versionned service.

param unversioned_service_name_fmt

pattern for name of unversioned service.

param unversioned_service_url_fmt

pattern for url of unversioned service.

param consul_token

If set, the consul token is sent on http api call.
```
